action.reasoning: Observation: the frontier-direction planner produced a large jump to 0.4259 official score with 0.66 success and much better outdoor/tunnel behavior; local subset score is 0.677. Analysis: this is a strong planner-only gain, but room success is still only 0.30 and room successful paths are long. The next best use of time is a cheap planner refinement focused specifically on room completion: detect low-progress/high-branching situations and increase commitment toward frontier clusters while penalizing tiny back-and-forth moves more aggressively. Since the checkpoint is unchanged and behavior is config/planner-driven, we can iterate quickly with local eval and submit immediately if it looks at least as good. Decision: patch planner with a room-recovery mode and stronger oscillation penalty, tune config for rooms, run local eval on a subset, then submit the same hybrid checkpoint for official eval.
